name: Torrelapaja - Berdejo - Torrijo de la Cañada — circular from Calatayud
name_original: Torrelapaja - Berdejo - Torrijo de la Cañada — circular desde Calatayud
region_intro: Road cycling around Calatayud offers diverse landscapes, from expansive tracks to forested areas and river valleys. The region is characterized by varied terrain, including areas with significant elevation changes and "Extreme Vineyard" status due to altitude and soil diversity. Cyclists can explore a network of routes that traverse this varied environment, providing a constantly changing backdrop for rides. The low population density ensures a tranquil cycling experience on its many secondary roads. Best road cycling routes around Calatayud The most popular road cycling route is Tierras de Frontera Calatayud Loop, a 43.3 miles (69.7 km) trail that takes 3 hours 8 minutes to complete. This moderate route explores the borderlands region around Calatayud. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Sierra de la Virgen via the Río Aranda — circular from Calatayud, a difficult 64.2 miles (103.3 km) path. This route features significant climbs through the Sierra de la Virgen and along the Río Aranda. Local road cyclists also love the Torrelapaja - Berdejo - Torrijo de la Cañada — circular from Calatayud, a 58.2 miles (93.6 km) trail leading through several villages, often completed in about 4 hours 5 minutes. Road cycling around Calatayud is defined by varied terrain, river valleys, and areas of significant elevation. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate rides to challenging ascents. The routes in Calatayud are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 5.0 stars from over 3 reviews. More than 647 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Calatayud's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many road cycling routes are available around Calatayud?
A: Calatayud and its surrounding area offer an extensive network of road cycling routes. You can find over 200 cycling routes, with 43 routes specifically curated on komoot, catering to various skill levels.

Q: What is the best time of year for road cycling in Calatayud?
A: The most popular months for road cycling in Calatayud are March and July. The region offers diverse landscapes that are enjoyable throughout much of the year, but these months are particularly favored by cyclists.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on road cycling routes in Calatayud?
A: Road cycling around Calatayud features highly varied terrain, from expansive tracks and forested areas to picturesque river valleys. You'll encounter routes with significant elevation changes, including areas known for their 'Extreme Vineyard' status due to altitude and soil diversity. The low population density ensures a tranquil experience on many secondary roads.

Q: Are there road cycling routes suitable for beginners in Calatayud?
A: Yes, Calatayud offers routes suitable for beginners. There are 3 easy road cycling routes available, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40335146" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Calatayud – ⭐️ Puerta de Terrer loop from Valdeherrera&lt;/a&gt;, which is 23.1 miles (37.1 km) long and features minimal elevation gain.

Q: Are there challenging road cycling routes for experienced riders?
A: Absolutely. For experienced riders seeking a challenge, Calatayud has 17 difficult road cycling routes. A notable option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e988418684/sierra-de-la-virgen-via-the-rio-aranda-circular-from-calatayud" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sierra de la Virgen via the Río Aranda — circular from Calatayud&lt;/a&gt;, a demanding 64.2 miles (103.3 km) path with significant climbs.

Q: Can I find circular road cycling routes in Calatayud?
A: Many of the road cycling routes in Calatayud are circular, allowing you to start and end in the same location. For example, the popular &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e991333019/tierras-de-frontera-calatayud-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tierras de Frontera Calatayud Loop&lt;/a&gt; is a 43.3 miles (69.7 km) circular route exploring the borderlands region.

Q: What natural features can I see while road cycling around Calatayud?
A: The region boasts diverse natural features. You can cycle through picturesque river valleys, witness the striking red-rock landscapes of the Sierra de Armantes, or visit the significant wetland of Gallocanta Lake, known for its migratory birds. While not directly on cycling routes, the enchanting Monasterio de Piedra Natural Park with its waterfalls is also nearby.

Q: Are there any interesting landmarks or historical sites along the routes?
A: Yes, Calatayud is rich in history and culture. You can explore charming villages, see examples of Mudejar architecture, or visit historical sites like the Roman ruins of Bílbilis. Some routes might pass through or near towns like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4394218" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ateca&lt;/a&gt; or &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4591078" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Maluenda&lt;/a&gt;, which offer their own unique character.

Q: What do other road cyclists enjoy the most about road cycling in Calatayud?
A: The road cycling experience in Calatayud is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 5.0 stars from over 3 reviews. Cyclists often praise the varied terrain, the tranquil experience on secondary roads due to low population density, and the diverse landscapes that provide a constantly changing backdrop for rides.

Q: Are there any routes that offer scenic viewpoints or summits?
A: Yes, several routes offer opportunities for scenic views. You might encounter climbs leading to summits like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5247466" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cruz de Armantes&lt;/a&gt; or &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2612188" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pico del Rayo&lt;/a&gt;, providing panoramic vistas of the surrounding landscapes. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e991310161/daroca-and-puerto-de-murero-by-the-camino-del-cid-cycling-route-circular-from-calatayud" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Daroca and Puerto de Murero by the Camino del Cid Cycling Route&lt;/a&gt; also offers beautiful scenery.

Q: Can I combine road cycling with other activities like wine tasting or relaxation?
A: Absolutely. Calatayud is a prominent wine region known for its Garnacha grapes, with many vineyards offering tours and tastings. After a day of cycling, you can also relax at one of the area's thermal spas, such as those in Alhama de Aragón, Paracuellos de Jiloca, or Jaraba, which provide therapeutic treatments.
tips: Located in the Manubles Valley we find another of the many charming towns with a lot of architecture and history in this area of Aragon. The imposing Castle stands out, of which the remains remain today, and the spectacular church of San Millán located on top of a rocky crag.

Torrijo de la Cañada is a town located in the Manubles river valley that will undoubtedly leave us speechless. In Torrijo de la Cañada there was an episode that left a mark in the history of Spain during the Celtiberian wars. We can also visit the Church of Nuestra Señora del Hortal, the Hermitage or the old bridge where we are. And on the hills that rise above the town, there are more than a hundred old wineries that give us an unusual and wonderful image.

Beautiful basilica in the city of Calatayud where the interior stands out with an altar and impressive baroque wooden altarpieces. It is the most important basilica of the order of the Holy Sepulcher of Jerusalem in Spain.
